invis+henchman+war machine on henchman again = 8 card deck :) Take THAT Dark memory villains, I dont HAVE A DISCARD PILE MUHUHAHAHAAH!! (probabbly burnt a bystander hero too early, may bite later :P )

Really didn't like the captain marvel cards. I thought joe fixit would be better but game one actually didn't help also saw no rares. Game two was better, no marvel cards and opened board with invisible woman rare, really put in work had her almost every ambush villan

column35: What a great game! After a really close call for an escape as the city had filled up, my decks slowly ramped up and allowed me to rake in bystanders while punching the Illuminati in their faces!
Gladiator Hulk mad Iluminati a bit less of a pain, but the master strike timing left me a few turns with nothing to do. PS - where are my sidekicks? 😭
I lost the first time around due to the requirement of final blow. It kind of comes down to luck in where those darn scheme twists are! Overall a good battle. Phew.

1st attempt was dismal. One of the most frustrating games I’ve ever played. Villain deck was front-loaded with Henchmen but I could not Outwit them to get the KOs for the life of me, despite each deck buying a mix of costs for that purpose. Whenever I was about to get a good combo together a Master Strike ruined my hand! I repeated the Heroes because I knew they could do better. 2nd attempt was much better, some very trim decks, with both hands using Unseen Rescue to get a mound of Bystanders, but alas too low on Attack to finish quickly, and the final Twist landed and finished me just as I had the Attack in hand for the final blow! Ah well, probably wasn’t fast enough for a good ranking anyway.
Got crushed, 2 more hits on the mastermind, my teammate's deck wasn't really functioning as planned :P
Game 2 felt like I was only ever able to recruit things, never defeat stuff. I got all the stars and almost nothing to use them on, not even Focus.
Hopeless might not be the right word, but discouraging certainly is. Not sure what the right combo of Heroes was. I'll be curious to see the rest of the pod's results.

This set up was very difficult for us. The amount of fight in the hero cards that were chosen for us was little to none. To much recruit and not enough fight. The scheme was tricky on leaving the large cost cards so they don't get Ko'd. Due to those ones having a higher fight value. Also never got a change to transform any other hero cards. For us it didn't work well.

column35: I think I got pretty lucky, half of my villain deck pulls were twist, strike, bystander so the city pressure wasn't much. MM mostly whiffed and I managed to hit really big fast with Beta Ray Bill, OG Angel, and Sif's Golden Apple. Fun set up and I enjoyed the heroes I picked!

Decided to go with the discard dream team of Angel and Beta Ray. Both games went pretty well but ended up being able to recruit the Beta rare in game 2 which made things go crazy. The final turn was up to 44 attack which was able to take out the 1 Villain in the city and hit the mastermind 3 times to end it.
NA
This curdled my melk

Went with some more tech in Forge and Beta Ray Bill. BRB also helped with the a artifact/worthy side as well. Thought I could do it faster the seconde time around, but it seemed that the early 5 cost Lady Sif made the difference in the first attempt. Also the Gamora rare is insane when activated every turn.

I thought we brought along the right heroes for the first game, but their cards didn't really show up in the HQ like we wanted. So we ran it back making a swap to Wolverine (X-Force) for more KO potential with Groot. We got luckier getting more of the cards we wanted in the second attempt, albeit without getting any Ultimates. My teammate had a strong setup with Wolverine and Groot cards getting rid of most of the basic starters and I focused on Gamora getting shards. We did a better job of keeping the shards off of Supreme Intelligence so his discards wouldn't hurt us much, but he still got us a few times.

These games were both so close to each other. This one was 3 turns faster but 0.2 lower vps/turn. Let's hope I submitted the right one. Was a bit of a shame that when choosing a powerful hero like Starlord he spends his time mostly hiding in the hero deck both games.
It was nice being able to keep to most of the same sets as last week

I really like to use the shards in games, so I was happy to see them appear in the tournament. I got to use some of the rare heroes, but wasn't really lucky with the draws to reach their full potential, which cost me 3 or 4 extra turns in both games. Great setup though! I was thinking about using Rocket or Punisher, but I was pretty satisfied with my choices after game 1, so didn't feel the need to change them.

Both games were a little slow unfortunately. In game 1 I hit the mastermind really quickly the first couple times (thank you Gamora's shards!) but a series of master strikes wrecked my hands and made the Supreme Intellgience's attack shoot up. Overall not too bad, but using Star-Lord and Forge this early might hurt me. I probably should have saved Forge for a more tech heavy setup, oh well. Not too fun of a setup but not terrible either. Real standard stuff.

Getting to hands generating 6 attack AND 6 buy at the same time was astral pain, but once I got to it game ended fast. Sending fear lords straight to Astral realm with Ancient one so you evade fighting them twice was secret tech

Unsolicited statistic: P1 final deck - 6 cards and an artifact in play, P2 final deck - 7 cards and an artifact in play. I was worried I wouldn't have enough cards to finish the game.
Fun, but stressful.
The first attempt the scheme presented no threat at all but we had 4 Ultimate/Epic/High cost heroes that clogged up the HQ delaying our win.

Really tough game and took a while to get my decks going. Found it difficult to manage being able to recruit and manage the Astral Plane at the start. Not sure my hero choices were the best but Doctor Voodoo’s Medallion helped manage the scheme.
Nightmare must have a headache bouncing back and forth to and from the Astral Plane. Pretty cool mechanic they made for him. Annoying, though, because it felt like I was fighting him twice as much.

We decided to add Attack Heroes and hoped to fight on the correct Scheme Twist. This works on 1 game out of 2, so it was not a very good plan. Black Widow and Iron Man did their thing, and we rescued lots of Bystanders.

I just finished my second run at Pod 16. I felt very confident about my hero choices, adding Mr. Fantastic and the Invisible Woman. My first run-through was a brutal fail (despite a late-game win) due to a run of Hydra Elites that overran the city right from the start of the game. The adapting masterminds seemed to know just when to change to keep me off balance.  I ended up with way too much recruit (nasty scheme) and it took me way too long to trim my deck enough to get Mr. Fantastic and Deathlok working. My second game, however, was much more lucky.  I recruited 3 copies of Deathlok's Reanimate into Service in the first three turns, and it was a steamroll from there. It was a devastating win. I was drawing my entire deck every turn at the end and demolishing everything in sight.  It was beautiful. The setup was well thought out, very thematic, and lots of fun. Thanks!
Our first game handed me a perfect Widow and Sue Storm deck, with a couple master strikes at the beginning doing nothing to us. It was great RNG.

column35: Fantastic Four always works so well together—we added Mr. Fantastic and Human Torch to team up with Thing…and…it was clobbering time!

I really don't like this scheme. Combined with the adapting mastermind there was a lot of unpredictability and lot of turns going to waste. First game was going really well. Had a clean city, quite large shield levels, got 4 out of 5 of the 7/8 cost cards (except for Nick Fury that would have been useless in that game). Then in 6 turn received 4 scheme twists and reached a very anti-climatic ending. (With 15 cards remaining in deck. Losing on 7th twist out of 8 is so baaad:). (Skrulls were shy, only 1 or 2 came appeared at all).
Second game went very stressful, always hoping for not drawing the next scheme twists and a lot of analysis paralysis (using focus and the extra recruit from hand ninjas or not, etc.) Besides thing+hulk actually Nick Fury turned out to be MVP here, his ultimate card defeated two of the four masterminds. Lot of KO here. Not much of shield level on the other hand. The ending was satisfying and had a few fun turns with 10-20 recruit + 10-20 attack in both of the games, but this setup is quite luck dependent. Yeah I also had a game-state with Zola having +19 power from the HQ and no master strike arriving for some turns in game one. So nice mechanics here with high cost, high damage heroes and skrull friends to gain them, but still it was very exhausting.

Game 1 I got stuck on a Scheme twist for recruit for far too long and unable to get any hits on the Mastermind at crucial attack steps. The Thing Rare kind of fell to the wayside and I didn't get to focus that Mister Fantastic as much.

Game 2 I had a much better gameplan of having one hand being recruit heavy.(My shield side) and my heavy hitter side. (Thing/Hulk), the only issue is I didn't see a lot of Mockingbird Uncommon cards till later in the game but with the help of Mockingbird's rare. We were able to take down the team before anymore damage could be done. Thing's Rare also had a chance to help in the limelight by getting it early from an attack heavy round being recruit.

Would have had more fun if I wasn't stuck on recruit with big attack heavy turns. RIP.

column35: I usually have a bit of trouble with Lady Deathstrike and the Prey mechanic, but this time was a bit easier which I didn't expect when I had a bonded Pretty Boy multiple times among others.  The extra henchmen group from the scheme was greatly appreciated, making 5's and 6's instead of much higher numbers.  My choices were made to maximize Shatterstar so Rictor's Unearth Tectonic Power was perfect to search and cost 5, and Nova was for more ranged and flexibility (and then he never showed up...).  Left side went Instinct, Right side went Ranged, and it wasn't the quickest fight but the city got cleaned up and I only hit the shuffle tactic twice.

I chose Moon Knight and Fantastic Four United because they could get extra bystander. Alas, Moon Knight's rare never showed in either game, so those bystanders never happened. In Attempt 1 All 3 uncommons of Fantastic Four Uniteds were bought and did get some extra bystanders, but overall it was a game that felt much harder than Attempt 2. Moon Knight's Tech potential was also needed/useful for coutnering one of Mandarin's Tactics.

I had kind of run into the potential issues with game 1 and maybe was also a bit more relaxed when I played game 2, so it felt easier. In Game 2 KO-ing cards with Moon Knights Commons actually worked and I soon had a deck without any recruit left, it didn't have too many good cards in it, but it was sufficient to defeat problems in the city and/or the Mastermind. Also the help of the Inifinity Gems was much better in game 2. The Reality Gems helped to put Master Strikes at the bottom as they are kind of a pain with Mandarin. One of the Rings (I don't know the name) was also very useful in getting another ring for free and getting an Infinity Gem (the one with + 2 recruit; Soul Stone?) for free. The setup was also kind of hard to beat the Scheme goals. There weren't that many targets for 5 cost cards and only 4 villains in the Villain Deck were worth 3 or more VP, so defeating them when required was also a puzzle. In Game 1 that puzzle took priority, but in Game 2 I luckily just outperformed game 1 by so much that it felt like a breeze in comparison and Evil Wins wasn't even close yet when the Final Blow was dealt.

Deck 1 with deckbuilding focused more on the recruiting part and got 3 rares (Super-Skrull, Ronin and Black Cat). Eventually Invisible Woman only rescued 2 Bystanders for 2 KO's, but it still felt like the better game to me. Because the setup was intentionally (I assume) made anti-synergistic/difficult I don't give it a 5/5 fun level, but for a game with Infinity Gems 4/5 is not bad in my book. Up to next week's challenge!

column35: Fun game!

1st attempt saw way too much Black Cat early on in the HQ, but she stayed hidden for the 2nd attempt. I guess you'd call it "Withholding Evidence" based on how often we ignored the scheme twist challenges so we could complete our turn as intended. Shards helped to grant the extra attack needed to hit the Mandarin or one of his 4 attack rings. Very fun setup, sharp contrast to our brutal one last week!
Interesting setup, Star Lord just didn't come to play very hard second round
I’ve never had so much success fiddling with the villain deck. Between the reality gem, hellcat, and psi-lord I was so aware what the next turn held. What a fun setup.

Normally this scheme isnt too bad multiplayer.  Swingy, but manageable  This scheme with the Mandarion ,two villain groups used here and buffed henchmen? Borderline unfair as twists 4,6,and 8 are extremely difficult to fulfil here.
Lots of fun, but wow tough. Our first set up had 7 schemes and 1 masterstrike out in the first 9 turns. And we shuffle it so well, but must have shuffled them back together. Thank again.
The gems made the "defeat something in the city worth 3vp or more" so difficult. Other than that it was a good challenging game.
I got destroyed by the scheme twist challenges.

This setup was punishingly difficult. Villain Groups and Henchmen had relatively high attack and low KO power. My go-to picks for KO power heroes were unavailable, so I just couldn't refine my decks. Super-Skrull and Ronin both have no cards granting Recruit points, so most turns my best choice for recruit was just more officers.
Lethal Legion + Infinity Gems were really unfortunate with this scheme. I lost twists 4, 6, and 8 just because all villains in the city were Gems (no VIP) or Locations (not technically villains). There was probably some bad luck here, but honestly I disliked this setup so much that I don't want to try again.

column35: Unfortunate early bystander kos forced me to play slowly, but it still went nice enough not to replay i think

Fun mastermind I haven’t played before. Tried on my second game to only save bystanders on one side to limit how many get KO’d by twists. Should have gone with Wolverine for the instinct to stop the twist and handle the wounds. Maybe pair with Hulk for synergy.

I wanted Instinct cards to protect against Scheme Twists, and after some waffling, ended up with General Okoye and Wolverine (X-Force) the first game. That gave the deck a pretty even distribution of colors, which minimized the amount of Empowering going on. The first game went fine but kind of slow (normal for me), but I recognized the power of Cheering Crowds to use all those Bystanders which could also keep Twists from ending the game. So the second game, I subbed out Wolverine for Gwenpool - similar amount of Instinct, more Cheering Crowds. And I think it worked! Second game was one of the fastest games I've played, and Player 1 ended up going Ranged-heavy with Cable's "Strike at the Heart of Evil" (love that card!) and Nova's "Interstellar Hero" (cheering crowds!) with almost no Instinct at all - since he was throwing back all his Bystanders to Cheering Crowds, he didn't really need to protect his Victory Pile from Scheme Twists. Player two got good mileage from General Okoye's "Lead the Dora Milaje" to burn Wounds and get lots of damage. I did get a pretty lucky Villain draw with a lot of early Henchmen and other Fight effects to KO my heroes - player two had basically no Starter Heroes left by the end of the game, which led to lots of combos. Best turn was something like 22 damage, hitting Klaw once, killing another Villain, and then electing not to take the Final Blow and instead hitting Klaw's "Cohesive Sound Construct" in the city again to rescue 5 more bystanders since player 1 was going to be able to finish things off with Cable.
I really loved this setup, and forgot how much I loved playing with Champions! Definitely going to request this set more often.
forge come out heavy early, so I was able to build a really strong tech tech, with some Shuri and Cable.

column35: This was great!  I was worried early as I didn't pay attention to the fact that bystanders in my Victory pile can still be KO'd.  But I was able to get Nova's cheering crowds in play on both sides, along with Domino's Instinct to keep the losses to a minimum.  It's always fun to pull off Forge's BFG and I managed it once here, taking out Klaw twice in one turn.  Shuri's common empowered by Tech became a star card with all the tech cards in the HQ.  I thought her Shock Net might play a bigger role in keeping Bystanders out of the HQ, but it was a non-factor.
Plan was to get as many yellow cards as possible to stave off Scheme twists but just didn't get them in either shuffle. Ended up just limiting buys to keep HQ static often. Creative setup.
Not a great strategy on my part. Tried to blitz it, should have added a bunch of instinct instead

Really a fun scenario it’ll get you quick if you are not careful or if you are unlucky three bystanders going out at the first of the game is a very unfortunate way to start. I loved the constant strategizing that was required for it. I have never played with cable before and I really like that character, I truly hate Carrion right now as he feasted on strike at the heart of evil both times I got the card before I could use it. Next time I just won’t kill him. Also, my wife is in a tourniquet because of all the wounds she received. I am lucky she didn’t bleed out.
Both games were surprisingly easy, but second game we got Namor to trigger and also 12 attack on one turn, and the other hand got doctor of beatdown and plenty of tech and strength.
Fun combination of characters.

P1 used Captain Britain as an engine to build up attack/recruit due to all the hero teams, and the Hulkbuster cards ended up being very helpful here. P2 used Namor and Joe Fixit to try to delete the many high attack villains so we could hit Iron Man. Luckily I got Namor's Imperius Rex card very early which helped keep Iron Man vulnerable (never did get that free Master Mind KO, but I tried!), and Joe Fixit's cards benefited from high VP villains to generate a lot of attack. Plenty of transformations fired off in both decks. One more scheme twist and I would have been sunk, though.
Pretty Rough, but LOTS of card draw

Picked a couple powerhouses for Game 1 and got destroyed by the Scheme so I decided to go wild for Game 2 and try to get some Villain Deck knowledge with Black Swan and Dr. Punisher. Honestly did not expect to pull out a Win considering half the Twists came out in the first 11 turns. The chosen Heroes didn't quite work out as expected, but did some decent damage with lots of Ranged and Instinct triggers; and Bruce/Hulkbuster on the other side did all the heavy lifting. Also managed to get rid of a Grievous Wound by KOing a Hero of 1 cost or more from the discard pile, and then immediately gained it back with Rise From the Ashes. All in all a decent setup, but I think Tony Stark should go back to driving school. >.<

Tale of 2 games. On the first attempt, I was in solid control from the jump and never let up. Couldn't get the sacrifice/ Phoenix Force Cyclops engine going like I was hoping, but other than that it went well. Game 2 was a complete disaster. Master Strikes and villain charges right off the bat made it difficult to control the city. I couldn't get enough recruit going to keep the Hulk deck thin. That was what did me in, but in the end it was only a matter of time.

It was fun, but we just had some bad luck. The first game I had some great cards early, but we also got two extra Scheme Twists from two Nighthawks getting pushed by two Whizzers right away before we could get our attack up. It was insane and unfortunate. The second we just were about a turn too late with the Scheme Twists. Not our best showing.

We have had a lot of fun playing the setups in this and previous tournaments. We feel like this setup was just not fun for either of us. The hero picks were extremely awkward, and the konshu henchmen were just such a bad choice given everything else in the setup. Like I said, usually we have a blast with your games, and for the sake of transparency, this was was definitely our least fun and would not like to play this one again lol

That was a fun game! I decided to go Bystander rescue as a way to mitigate ROTLD with my hero choices, and it worked great! Left side was a “Good Stuff” deck that KOd down to 1 grey card, with some just huge turns, and even triggered Captain Marvel’s rare twice! (Lucky to grab her before Goblin could get to her). Right side was a pure Strength deck that smashed to victory. Thing’s Crime Stopper was awesome on this side. Shielding the city from zombies with innocent Bystanders is a great strategy, hahaha. A bit high on escapes and turns, but honestly happy to have won!

This game was so fun but
I wish I didn't start it so late in the evening, as it was a long game! Rise of the Living Dead was the trickiest thing to navigate as it resulted in some heroes escaping by accelerating the villains in the city...had to try and defeat either Sinister Six or Subterranea villains to ensure they were on top of VP pile.
Was happy with one of my teams (included Mr Fantastic and Kitty Pryde) but thought the synergies with my Team 2 (Carol, some Ghost Rider and Bishop) really fizzled..maybe I played it wrong but Team 2 never was able to generate enough attack....Team one had a massive 34 Attack turn with 4 x Unstable Molecules and 2 x Gigantic Hands...the highlight of the game for me!!:)
Won both games, did better on the second attempt, the heroes I chose made the game to be really simple but still fun.

2nd game ended on less turns + more total vp + more bystanders.

Most fun I've had in a long time with just the core set. Wish I had more tech heroes for Ultron than on the second attempt though

column2: Doom Doom Doom Doom, a Killbot in My Room
column35: Elected not to bring any tech heroes, as Doom's master strike effectively makes his Doombots less likely to kill something useful. Instead brought Cyclops to add Strength for both Radiation Fight effects and Rogue's trigger, and then Gambit, since everybody was X-Men!!, so his card draw was pretty much guaranteed for either side.

I needed to learn the basics and then add in stuff from there. I look forward to to the next week. This was sooooo much fun!! Thanks for running this!!
